name: authority_cleanup_dispatch_packets
outcome_goal: Package the authority transition into bounded, repeatable dispatch packets and review sweeps so migration, backfill, and discovery cleanup can be re-run without chat context.
anti_requirements: Do not leave packet definitions implicit in chat history.
Do not use one-off shell commands as the only migration path.
Do not conflate registry authority with runtime lifecycle truth.

jobs.label: dispatch_packetization_packet
jobs.outcome_goal: Produce the bounded queue packets and review sweeps that carry the authority migration forward.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: No monolithic cleanup queue and no ambiguous packet ownership.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Package the authority-cleanup transition into bounded, reusable dispatch artifacts.

TASK:
Create or refine the queue packets, review sweeps, and reference docs required to run the authority-cleanup program without depending on chat context. Keep each packet owned by one seam, make registry-path dependencies explicit, and ensure review packets search specifically for surviving bypasses, duplicate seeds, and silent fallback behavior.

CONSTRAINTS:
Do not widen packet scopes across multiple major design decisions. Do not use filesystem queue lists as runtime discovery. Keep packet ownership aligned to the roadmap child items.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Dispatch artifacts under `artifacts/workflow/authority_cleanup/` that cleanly map roadmap items to runnable packets and review sweeps.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- Every authority-cleanup child item has a concrete packet ref.
- Packet scopes are narrow and registry paths are explicit.
- Review sweeps are called out separately from build packets.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not implement the runtime code changes themselves in this packet.
